news.t: AI & “Ex-China” Rewriting the Emerging Markets ETF Playbook
The Ex-China Files: ETFs to Watch Amid Trump's High-Stakes Visit
Emerging Market Funds That Exclude China Are Beating the Index
5 Emerging Market ETFs That Limit China Investments

holdings.infoBox: XCEM is an equity ETF with a total of 461 individual holdings. The top holdings are Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company stock at 15.58%, Samsung Electronics at 8.45%, SK hynix at 3.83%, MediaTek at 1.64%, and Delta Electronics at 1.54%.

overview.description: The Columbia EM Core ex-China ETF (XCEM) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index that provides broad equity exposure to emerging markets excluding China. XCEM was launched on Sep 2, 2015 and is issued by Columbia Threadneedle.

data.title: Emerging Market Funds That Exclude China Are Beating the Index
data.text: Ex-China funds are attracting assets as geopolitical tensions with the U.S. grab headlines and China underperforms other emerging market countries.

data.title: 5 Emerging Market ETFs That Limit China Investments
data.text: Emerging market exchange-traded funds that limit exposure to China may be a better bet for investors as China's stocks get battered.
